# Scoop manifest — generated by GoReleaser

There is no hand-maintained manifest here anymore (the previous
`multiai.json` carried `PLACEHOLDER_SHA256` hashes and referenced a
`windows_arm64` build that does not exist, audit v0.2.1 finding #4).

The manifest is **generated at release time** by GoReleaser (see the
`scoops` section of `multiai-go/.goreleaser.yaml`) with the real SHA256 of
the Windows archive. On every `v*` tag it lands in:

```
multiai-go/dist/scoop/multiai.json
```

## Publication

`skip_upload: true` until the bucket exists. To publish:

1. Manual (first releases): copy the generated `multiai.json` from the
   release run's `dist/` into a `lrochetta/scoop-bucket` repository.
2. Automated: create the bucket repo, add a `TAP_GITHUB_TOKEN` repo secret
   (a PAT with write access to the bucket — the default `GITHUB_TOKEN`
   cannot push to another repository), reference it via
   `repository.token: "{{ .Env.TAP_GITHUB_TOKEN }}"` and set
   `skip_upload: false` in `.goreleaser.yaml`.

Users then install with:

```powershell
scoop bucket add lrochetta https://github.com/lrochetta/scoop-bucket
scoop install multiai
```
